data.intent_description: Investigate two interface operational state changes: R1 Serial0/1/0 moved from up to lower-layer-down while admin state remains up; R2 Serial0/1/0 moved from up to down and admin state changed to down. Goal is to determine cause and whether remediation is required to restore expected operational state.

root_causes.cause: R2 Serial0/1/0 is administratively shut, causing that interface to be admin/down and oper/down on R2.
evidence: get_running_config (R2): Serial0/1/0 contains "shutdown"
get_interface_status (R2 Serial0/1/0): "admin_state": "down", "oper_state": "down", "last_change": "2026-05-26T09:40:51.000689+00:00"
OSPF (R2): Serial0/1/0 reported as interface_state "Down" with no neighbors

root_causes.cause: R1 Serial0/1/0 shows oper_state=lower-layer-down while admin_state=up because the remote peer (R2) is administratively down/unavailable, so the physical/line carrier is lost on R1.
evidence: get_interface_status (R1 Serial0/1/0): "admin_state": "up", "oper_state": "lower-layer-down", "last_change": "2026-05-26T09:40:54.000188+00:00"
get_interface_status (R2 Serial0/1/0) timestamped change ~3 seconds earlier at "2026-05-26T09:40:51.000689+00:00" (sequence consistent with R2 being shut then R1 losing carrier)
OSPF (R1): Serial0/1/0 listed as Down with no neighbors

OSPF (R2): Serial0/1/0 reported as interface_state "Down" with no neighbors

data.intent_description: R2 Serial0/1/0 is administratively shut causing R2 oper/down and R1 oper=lower-layer-down (loss of carrier). Goal: restore expected operational state and adjacency by bringing R2 Serial0/1/0 administratively up.

plan.problem: R2 Serial0/1/0 is administratively down (shutdown) causing the interface to be oper/down on R2 and causing R1 Serial0/1/0 to show lower-layer-down (loss of carrier).
plan.fix_summary: Remove the administrative shutdown on R2 Serial0/1/0 (bring interface administratively up) to restore physical carrier and neighbor adjacency.

messages.content:
What was observed
- R2 Serial0/1/0 was administratively down; R1 Serial0/1/0 was administratively up but oper=lower-layer-down. OSPF on the R1–R2 link was down.

Diagnosis (root cause)
- R2 Serial0/1/0 had shutdown configured (admin down) causing the physical carrier loss and the R1 interface to go operationally down.

Changes made
- On R2, interface Serial0/1/0 was enabled (administratively brought up).

Verification
- Passed. After the change: R2 Serial0/1/0 admin=up oper=up; R1 Serial0/1/0 admin=up oper=up; OSPF adjacency on the R1–R2 serial link is established (neighbor state full).

Remaining issues
- None.
